content.claim: Reform-sympathetic Hungarian experts identified that structural selection cannot be eliminated without first addressing underlying disparities in school quality, as families seek selective tracks due to distrust in local school quality—indicating a sequencing/precondition problem for implementation
content.context: Fresh live research through the Implementation planning lens
limitations: This is expert opinion on implementation prerequisites rather than empirical evidence of implementation failure
No quantitative analysis of how much quality equalization would be needed before selection could be safely removed
Contested whether sequencing (quality first vs. selection removal) is a technical or political constraint
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
